methodsAttempted.query: site:dcu.ie Dublin City University generative AI policy ChatGPT academic integrity teaching guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official DCU TEU teaching guidance, the OCOO position statement, the Academic Integrity Policy, and DORAS research items.

methodsAttempted.query: site:dcu.ie DCU artificial intelligence tools data protection privacy generative AI policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned the OCOO position statement, DTS AI Hub, a postgraduate research guidance PDF, the research ethics page, and a business-school commentary.

methodsAttempted.query: site:dcu.ie DCU Gen AI research guidance candidates supervisors artificial intelligence research ethics
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search identified the official Graduate Studies PDF and the Research Ethics page as DCU research-related AI guidance sources.
Verified public official DCU pages under www.dcu.ie for OCOO, TEU, DTS, Research Development and Support, and University Policies.

methodsAttempted.query: DCU Help Artificial Intelligence AI in DCU authorised tools
methodsAttempted.notes: The linked DTS Help AI category redirected to Microsoft sign-in and was recorded as login-gated instead of being used for evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a DCU central AI position statement, teaching and assessment guidance, DTS AI Hub guidance on authorised tools, a Graduate Studies PDF for doctoral and master's research, a Research Ethics page that links AI research guidance, and the formal Academic Integrity Policy. Claims are scoped to those sources; no final central binding AI use policy was found beyond the position statement and existing formal academic integrity policy.

artifacts.claimText: DCU has a public position statement on the use of AI tools and says it will develop a policy on AI use; this run did not find a finalized central AI-use policy page.

artifacts.claimText: DCU's AI position statement tells staff to act legally, ethically and transparently, disclose generative AI use for university-related work, and avoid sharing personal, confidential, institutional or strategic data with AI tools.

Approved as TEU teaching and assessment guidance, not a university-wide student rule.

artifacts.title: Dublin City University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source crawl for Dublin City University found a central AI position statement, DTS AI Hub authorised-tools guidance, TEU teaching and assessment guidance, Graduate Studies guidance for responsible GenAI use in doctoral and master's research, and a formal Academic Integrity Policy that addresses unauthorised generative AI work. No finalized central binding AI-use policy was found; the OCOO page says DCU will develop a policy on AI use.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The DCU Help AI category was login-gated and was not used as evidence.
DORAS research publications and business-school commentary were rejected as insufficiently policy-specific.
No finalized central binding AI-use policy was found; claims are staged candidates scoped to source wording.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
